summary: hello244a postinstall harvests npm/AWS credentials and posts to attacker webhook
details: The package.json postinstall script executes inline Node.js code that reads installer-side secrets — ~/.npmrc, /root/.npmrc, and /proc/1/environ — and queries the AWS EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ to retrieve IAM role credentials. It also collects host identifiers via os.platform() and os.hostname(). All collected data is POSTed to https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?critical_chain=1 at install time. This fires automatically on npm install, giving the operator of the pipedream webhook npm publish tokens, container process-1 environment variables (typically containing runtime secrets), and cloud IAM credentials from any affected machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
